proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence theorem for local-projection (LP) versus structural-vector-autoregression (SVAR) identification of structural impulse responses under non-Gaussian innovations and partial sign restrictions. Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica) established that LP and SVAR estimate the same population impulse response under finite-order linear VAR data-generating processes when both use the same identifying restrictions; Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf (Econometrica, forthcoming) extend the equivalence to local projections under misspecification of lag length. The flagship question: characterize the sharp boundary at which LP and SVAR identify distinct structural impulse responses when innovations are non-Gaussian with bounded higher-cumulant restrictions a la Gourieroux-Monfort-Renne (2017) and Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen (2017), and when the structural impact matrix is restricted only by a partial sign/zero pattern. The kernel claim is a closed-form algebraic non-equivalence theorem: under non-Gaussian innovations with bounded fourth cumulant gap, the LP-IRF and SVAR-IRF identify distinct functionals of the structural-shock distribution unless a cumulant-matching restriction is jointly imposed, recovering Plagborg-Moller-Wolf equivalence at the Gaussian limit.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains the banked parent eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1, whose proposed kernel used a single cumulant-envelope support loss chi_w(P). The v3 upgrade keeps the parent kernel but adds the mechanism-axis delta: chi_w(P) is decomposed into sign-exposure, common-impact-pasting, and cumulant-label channels, with an explicit parent-collapse theorem.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are PlagborgMollerWolf2021 for common-restriction LP/VAR equivalence, GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 and LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 for non-Gaussian SVAR labels, Wolf2022 for sign masquerading, CarrieroMarcellinoTornese2024 for blended identification, and DrautzburgWright2023 for independence-refined sign-restricted VAR sets. The gap is a support-function mechanism frontier connecting these ingredients, not another statement that adding a constraint shrinks a set."}
seed_list: Parent v1 has one margin chi_w(P); this upgrade conjectures a three-channel mechanism decomposition into row-exposure, cumulant-label residual, and pasting defect.
Parent v1 assumes primitive separation; this upgrade conjectures an iff frontier where strict LP/SVAR non-equivalence holds exactly when support exposure and cumulant/pasting separation jointly hold.
Parent v1 lacks a closed operational threshold; this upgrade conjectures tau_w^*(P), a critical mechanism threshold lower-bounding the support gap and reducing to chi_w(P) when collapsed.
Parent v1 treats sign restrictions as one relaxation; this upgrade uses Wolf2022 shock masquerading to isolate the sign-cone exposure channel from the non-Gaussian cumulant-label channel.

seed_details.one_line: Parent v1 has one margin chi_w(P); this upgrade conjectures a three-channel mechanism decomposition into row-exposure, cumulant-label residual, and pasting defect.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith parent proposal
anchor_paper.claim: no axis-b non-equivalence threshold derived in closed form; primitive margin remained assumed

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not a generic weakening; it isolates mechanisms bundled by the parent and names operational quantities.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from set inclusion because cumulant matching is just an added constraint.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: set inclusion gives no decomposition into E_w, L_delta4, and P_paste, nor an iff boundary for which mechanism drives the support loss.

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline must name operational quantities, not just a qualitative mechanism story.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patched by making E_w(P), L_delta4(P), P_paste(P), and tau_w^*(P) the focal objects.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims decompose-then-correct but does not propose a corrected estimator.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: This is exact-identification, not panel estimation; the correction is a diagnostic decomposition, so M3/M7 carry the flagship burden.

adversarial_referee.instance: Parent gap may be only a derivation failure, not a literature gap.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Cross-linked gaps cite PlagborgMollerWolf2021, DrautzburgWright2023, and non-Gaussian SVAR papers; no paper decomposes this margin.

seed_details.one_line: Parent v1 assumes primitive separation; this upgrade conjectures an iff frontier where strict LP/SVAR non-equivalence holds exactly when support exposure and cumulant/pasting separation jointly hold.

anchor_paper.author: Mikkel Plagborg-Moller and Christian K. Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: LPs and VARs estimate the same population impulse responses when the same identifying restrictions are imposed, including sign restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not T=2-to-general or iid-to-Markov; it targets the common-restriction boundary where non-Gaussian restrictions are not imposed on LP rows.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from PlagborgMollerWolf2021 because the LP can impose the same restrictions as the SVAR.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the conjecture characterizes when the sign-only LP relaxation fails to paste into one cumulant-diagonalizing shock law.

adversarial_referee.instance: The proof sketch may only show sufficiency for strict gap, not necessity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 is explicitly phrased as iff and flags boundary cases: response-flatness and cumulant-compatible LP maximizers.

adversarial_referee.instance: PMW may be described as failing, when it only assumes common restrictions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Proposal states PMW is recovered under common restrictions and does not attribute an invalid shortcut to them.

adversarial_referee.instance: No critical threshold would cap the iff result to field tier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 adds tau_w^*(P) as the named threshold.

seed_details.one_line: Parent v1 lacks a closed operational threshold; this upgrade conjectures tau_w^*(P), a critical mechanism threshold lower-bounding the support gap and reducing to chi_w(P) when collapsed.

anchor_paper.author: Alain Guay
anchor_paper.claim: Higher unconditional moments yield local SVAR identification rank conditions and tests, including partial local identification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not a generic computability add-on; it converts cumulant residual plus pasting defect into a support-gap lower bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Guay2021 rank conditions because rank failure already measures higher-moment identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Guay gives local identification rank checks, not an LP/sign versus SVAR/sign support-function threshold.

adversarial_referee.instance: Threshold tau_w^*(P) might be noncomputable if lambda_w(P) is abstract.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Proposal defines lambda_w(P) as a local response modulus and flags possible Holder replacement in scope flags.

adversarial_referee.instance: A lower bound is only sufficient and may not characterize the boundary.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 is paired with Conjecture 1's iff frontier and asks for sharpness along boundary sequences.

adversarial_referee.instance: Read2022 algorithms might already compute the same support gap.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Read2022 handles sign/zero rotation feasibility, not cumulant-distance support lower bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Parent v1 treats sign restrictions as one relaxation; this upgrade uses Wolf2022 shock masquerading to isolate the sign-cone exposure channel from the non-Gaussian cumulant-label channel.

anchor_paper.author: Christian K. Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: Sign-restricted VARs can suffer shock masquerading, where sign restrictions do not isolate the intended structural shock.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it does not merely apply Wolf's diagnosis; it embeds the sign-channel in a cumulant-matching LP/SVAR decomposition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Wolf2022 because shock masquerading already explains sign-restriction failure.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Wolf does not combine masquerading with non-Gaussian cumulant diagonalization or LP/SVAR support comparisons.

adversarial_referee.instance: A referee may ask why only one shock label j is studied.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Locked parameters fix labelled shock j; family extension across labels can follow once the one-label boundary is proved.

adversarial_referee.instance: Wolf2022 is not a non-Gaussian SVAR paper.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Used only for the sign-exposure mechanism, while non-Gaussian labels are anchored to GMR/LMS/Guay.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could remain a qualitative diagnosis without a scalar.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: E_w(P) is the named scalar sign-cone exposure score.

proposed_from.literature_map: Parent map reused and spot-verified: PlagborgMollerWolf2021 is the Econometrica common-restriction LP/VAR equivalence anchor; DrautzburgWright2023 is the Journal of Econometrics independence-refined sign-restricted VAR anchor; GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 and LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 are the non-Gaussian SVAR/ICA identification anchors; Guay2021 gives higher-moment rank conditions; Read2022 gives sign/zero algorithms. New frontier anchors added for the mechanism upgrade: Wolf2022 names shock masquerading under sign restrictions, CarrieroMarcellinoTornese2024 studies blended SVAR identification combining set restrictions with stronger shock information, and LutkepohlStrohsal2026 studies non-Gaussian SVAR shock-transmission settings where common-law diagnostics matter.

banked_reason: Flagship-mechanism upgrade D-0.5 NO-PASS after pivot budget exhausted (4/4); three distinct angles (2 v3, 3 v1, 4 v3) reached publishability_tier:flagship with upgrade_delta_verdict:delivered but each was killed by statement-level well-posedness flags (C-sanity tie-break in Theorem 1; C-wellposed scalar-curvature/sign-cone normalisation; C-coherence tautological iff) that the reviewer recommended patching and rerunning; the kernel survives but the pipeline's revise-cap + reject-on-soundness policies foreclosed iteration.
